the_signal.headline: Error handling in LLM tool calling moves from edge case to architecture
the_signal.body: n8n's guide on LLM tool-calling error handling reveals that failure classification, smart retries, and circuit breakers are now table-stakes for production agentic systems. GTM engineers building AI revenue systems need this wired before agents touch real customer data — a single unhandled tool failure cascades into lost deals and compliance violations.

tool_drop.name: Glaze by Raycast
tool_drop.what: AI-powered interface customization layer for Raycast extending command capabilities.
tool_drop.why: If your GTM ops team lives in Raycast, this bridges the gap between AI automation and workflow — less context switching means faster agent iteration cycles.

thread_of_day.title: Agentic coding notes from Galapagos Island
thread_of_day.takeaway: Agentic loops expose the difference between optimization and synthesis — agents excel at iteration but struggle with novel problem framing, a hard lesson for founders betting on autonomous revenue workflows.

quote.text: When a review run falls short, this workflow catches it and proposes a fix to the skill, then writes the improved instructions back once you approve the diff.
quote.source: n8n Blog — Build Self-Improving Agent Skills

whats_moving.context: n8n's architecture guide signals that teams are moving from 'hope retries work' to designing explicit fallback trees and circuit breakers — this is now non-negotiable for production deployments.

whats_moving.topic: Vector database selection for RAG at scale
whats_moving.context: The 10-database comparison from n8n suggests GTM teams are now benchmarking metadata filtering and overhead — sign that RAG-powered agents are hitting scale limits and demanding architectural decisions.

whats_moving.topic: AI maturity frameworks for leadership buy-in
whats_moving.context: n8n's 5-level framework gives founders a language to explain to boards why 'we use ChatGPT' isn't strategy — GTM leaders need this to unlock budget for real agentic infrastructure.

act_on_this: Map your agent's failure modes against n8n's error-handling checklist (classification, retries, fallbacks, circuit breakers) — if you can't articulate how your system recovers from a tool-call timeout, you're not production-ready for customer-facing workflows.

numbers.stat: 5 distinct maturity levels across 4 dimensions in AI governance frameworks
numbers.context: This structure lets GTM teams escape the 'we're doing AI' trap and actually benchmark progress — critical for justifying continued investment in agentic systems to finance.
